name: Washington Basic Food (SNAP)
description: Washington's SNAP program, called Basic Food, provides monthly benefits on an EBT card to help low-income individuals and families buy groceries. Benefits can be used at most grocery stores, farmers markets, and some online retailers.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% of the federal poverty level for most households
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Washington Apple Health (Medicaid)
description: Apple Health is Washington's Medicaid program providing free or low-cost health coverage. It covers doctor visits, hospital stays, prescriptions, mental health services, dental, and vision for eligible residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant individuals, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 312% FPL; pregnant individuals up to 193% FPL
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in over 200 languages

name: Washington Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who have lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Benefits are based on previous earnings and can last up to 26 weeks.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own and meet work history requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Benefits are calculated based on wages earned in the base year, up to a weekly maximum
languages: English, Spanish

name: Washington State Housing Finance Commission
description: Provides affordable housing programs including down payment assistance, rental assistance, and financing for affordable housing development. Helps first-time homebuyers and low-income renters across Washington.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income homebuyers and renters in Washington State
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program and county; generally targets households below 80% of area median income
languages: English, Spanish

name: Northwest Justice Project
description: Washington's largest publicly funded legal aid organization providing free civil legal assistance to low-income people. Handles cases involving housing, family law, public benefits, consumer issues, and immigration.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Washington residents with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: WorkSource Washington
description: Washington's statewide network of employment centers offering job search assistance, resume help, career counseling, training programs, and connections to employers. Services are free for all job seekers.
eligibilitySummary: All Washington job seekers and employers
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Washington LIHEAP - Energy Assistance
description: The Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helps low-income households pay heating and cooling bills. Administered locally through community action agencies, it provides one-time payments directly to energy providers.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 150% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% FPL or 60% of state median income
languages: English, Spanish

name: Washington 211
description: A free, confidential service connecting people to local resources for food, housing, utilities, health care, and other essential services. Available 24/7 by phone, text, or online search.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Washington seeking information about community resources
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: Northwest Harvest
description: Washington's leading statewide hunger relief agency distributing food to over 400 food banks, meal programs, and shelters across the state. Provides free groceries with no income requirements or ID needed.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance; no income requirements or ID needed
languages: English, Spanish

name: Food Lifeline
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving western Washington. Rescues and distributes food to over 350 food banks, shelters, and meal programs across 26 counties.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in western Washington
languages: English, Spanish

name: Second Harvest Inland Northwest
description: A Feeding America partner food bank serving eastern Washington and northern Idaho. Distributes millions of pounds of food annually through a network of over 250 partner agencies.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in eastern Washington
languages: English, Spanish

name: Washington Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Provides services to Washington veterans and their families including benefits assistance, claims support, long-term care, employment help, and connections to federal VA programs.
eligibilitySummary: Washington State veterans and their family members
languages: English, Spanish

name: Washington Crisis Connections
description: Provides 24/7 crisis support via phone, text, and chat for anyone experiencing emotional distress, suicidal thoughts, or mental health crisis. Connects callers with local resources and follow-up support.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Washington experiencing a mental health or emotional crisis
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Washington Working Connections Child Care
description: Subsidized child care program helping low-income families afford quality child care while working, attending school, or participating in approved activities. Covers licensed child care, family home care, and some in-home care.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children under 13 who are working or in approved activities
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 60% of state median income to start; can continue up to 85% SMI
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Washington State Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF)
description: Provides temporary cash assistance and support services to low-income families with children. Includes employment preparation, job search assistance, and other supportive services to help families achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children under 18
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below state TANF limits which vary by family size
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Washington WIC Program
description: The Women, Infants, and Children program provides nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Aging and Long-Term Support Administration (ALTSA)
description: Provides services for older adults and people with disabilities including in-home care, adult day services, residential care, and caregiver support. Helps people stay independent in their communities.
eligibilitySummary: Adults 65+ and adults with disabilities who need long-term care services
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; Medicaid-funded services have income limits
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Washington State Ferries - Reduced Fare Program
description: Provides discounted ferry fares for seniors, people with disabilities, and Medicare card holders on Washington State Ferries routes. An important transportation assistance resource in the Puget Sound region.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors 65+, people with disabilities, and Medicare card holders
hours: Varies by ferry route and schedule
languages: English

name: Paratransit and Special Needs Transportation
description: Washington provides coordinated transportation services for seniors, people with disabilities, and low-income individuals through regional transit agencies and community transportation providers.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors, people with disabilities, and low-income individuals with limited transportation options
languages: English, Spanish

name: Washington Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounted phone and internet service to qualifying low-income households. Participants receive a monthly discount on phone or broadband service from participating carriers.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households or participants in qualifying assistance programs like SNAP or Medicaid
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% FPL or enrollment in qualifying federal assistance programs
languages: English, Spanish

name: Washington State Bar Association Lawyer Referral Service
description: Connects people with attorneys for a low-cost initial consultation. Can help find lawyers for a wide range of civil and criminal legal issues across Washington State.
eligibilitySummary: Any Washington resident seeking legal assistance
languages: English, Spanish

name: Washington State Opportunity Scholarship
description: Provides scholarships to low- and middle-income Washington students pursuing eligible high-demand STEM, healthcare, or trade degrees and credentials at Washington colleges and universities.
eligibilitySummary: Washington residents with family income at or below 125% of state median income pursuing eligible degrees
incomeLimitNotes: Family income must be at or below 125% of state median income
languages: English, Spanish

name: Washington College Grant
description: Washington's largest state financial aid program provides grants covering tuition at eligible colleges and universities for low-income students. No repayment required.
eligibilitySummary: Washington residents with family income at or below 100% of state median income attending eligible institutions
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 100% of state median income for maximum award
languages: English, Spanish

name: Washington Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home weatherization improvements to reduce energy costs for low-income households. Services include insulation, air sealing, heating system repair or replacement, and energy audits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters at or below 200% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% FPL
languages: English, Spanish

name: Washington Emergency Rental Assistance Program
description: Provides rental assistance to tenants who are behind on rent and at risk of eviction. Funds are paid directly to landlords and utility providers on behalf of eligible tenants.
eligibilitySummary: Renters who are behind on rent and at risk of eviction due to financial hardship
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 80% of area median income
languages: English, Spanish

name: Seattle Human Services - Emergency Assistance
description: The City of Seattle provides emergency financial assistance for rent, utilities, and other basic needs to Seattle residents facing financial crisis. Multiple programs available through community-based organizations.
eligibilitySummary: Seattle residents experiencing financial crisis and at risk of homelessness
incomeLimitNotes: Generally income at or below 50% of area median income
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Seattle/King County Coalition on Homelessness
description: Provides information about shelter, transitional housing, and permanent supportive housing options in the Seattle and King County area. Connects individuals and families experiencing homelessness to available resources.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness in King County
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Tacoma-Pierce County Health Department - Community Resources
description: Provides health services, immunizations, WIC, family planning, and connections to community resources for Tacoma and Pierce County residents. Also offers environmental health and disease prevention services.
eligibilitySummary: Tacoma and Pierce County residents
incomeLimitNotes: Many services are free or low-cost based on income
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Spokane Neighborhood Action Partners (SNAP)
description: A community action agency serving Spokane County providing emergency services, energy assistance, housing stabilization, early childhood education, and self-sufficiency programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in Spokane County
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 200% FPL
languages: English, Spanish

name: Washington State Immigrant and Refugee Commission
description: Supports immigrant and refugee communities in Washington by connecting them to resources, advocating for policies, and helping navigate state services. Provides information in multiple languages.
eligibilitySummary: Immigrants and refugees in Washington State
languages: English, Spanish, and multiple other languages

name: Washington Disability Rights
description: Provides free legal advocacy and information to people with disabilities in Washington. Handles issues including abuse and neglect, discrimination, education, employment, housing, and access to services.
eligibilitySummary: People with disabilities in Washington State
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Washington 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline
description: Provides free, confidential support for people in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Call or text 988 to connect with a trained crisis counselor 24/7. Previously known as the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, mental health crisis, or emotional distress
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: Washington Passport to Careers
description: Provides financial aid and support services for current and former foster youth, unaccompanied homeless youth, and youth with involvement in the juvenile justice system to pursue postsecondary education.
eligibilitySummary: Current and former foster youth, homeless youth, and youth involved in juvenile justice system
languages: English, Spanish

name: Washington Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Provides crisis support, safety planning, and referrals for survivors of domestic violence. Connects callers with local shelters, legal advocacy, and support services across Washington State.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, dating violence, or sexual assault
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available
